title: New European driving license
body:
Yesterday I lost my wallet and I have to request a duplicate of all my documentation, let me see if my driving license already complies with the new format. As of today, all licenses issued will have the European format and the rest will be updated as they are duplicated, renewed, etc. This measure is part of a broader package of measures, the Third EU Directive on driving permits, which aims to strengthen freedom of movement, combat fraud in permits, and improve road safety throughout the EU. Here are some of its innovations:

### **A single driving license format for all Europeans:**

-   All new European driving permits will be issued according to a new format, that of a plastic "credit card", with a photograph and standard requirements on information, easy to recognize and read throughout the EU.
-   Existing permits are not affected, but will be replaced by the new format at the time of renewal, or at the latest in 2033. The European driving permit can be adapted to incorporate national symbols, as each Member State decides.

### **Greater security and combating bad practices:**

-   The new driving license includes a series of security measures so that it cannot be tampered with and to prevent counterfeiting.
-   Furthermore, it is backed by the creation of a European electronic data exchange system to facilitate the exchange of information between national administrations. In this way, the process of managing driving permits will be simplified for people who change their residence from one Member State to another. It will also be of great help in prohibiting "driving permit tourism" and fraud, for example, the implementation of the new and more restrictive prohibition that one Member State issue a permit to someone to whom another Member State has withdrawn, suspended, or restricted their permit.

### **Periodic renewal of permits**

Although in Spain the license renewal was done every 10 years, in other EU countries such as Belgium, the license was issued for life. Now, mandatory renewal is established for both motorcycle and car drivers every 10 to 15 years. As for permits for bus and truck drivers, they must be renewed every five years and a medical examination will be required for their renewal.

### **Protection of vulnerable road users**

The European driving license system strengthens the protection of the most vulnerable categories of road users, and includes the following:

-   A higher minimum age for direct access (through practical and theoretical tests) to permits for the most powerful motorcycles, from the current 21 years to 24. This will be a measure that will be carried out progressively.
-   Mopeds constitute a new category of vehicle and candidates for a moped permit will be required from now on to pass a knowledge control test.
-   Minimum standards for driving license examiners
-   Driving license examiners must comply with minimum standards regarding their initial qualification and their periodic training. This measure will introduce quality control in the new system.

[More information at ec.europa.eu >](http://ec.europa.eu/spain/index_es.htm)
